summary.intent: Address a CodeRabbit security review comment on PR #2676 by adding SHA256 checksum verification and proper install permissions for the ORAS CLI installation in the container image.
summary.outcome: Applied the security hardening fix to Containerfile.base (added ORAS_SHA256 ARG, sha256sum verification, and install -m 0755), committed with pre-commit hooks enabled, and pushed to the PR branch.

repo: The art-tools repository has mandatory pre-commit hooks that must not be bypassed with --no-verify
Containerfile.base follows a security pattern of SHA256 verification for downloaded binaries (used for opm, cosign, and now oras)
ORAS checksums are published in oras_<version>_checksums.txt on the GitHub releases page

code.finding: ORAS installation block required hardening: ARG ORAS_SHA256 declaration, curl -fsSLo for explicit downloads, sha256sum -c verification before extraction, and install -m 0755 instead of mv for proper permissions

workflow: Container image downloads should be verified with official checksums from upstream release pages
GNU sha256sum expects two spaces between digest and filename in checksum files
Use install -m 0755 instead of mv to explicitly set executable permissions on binaries

friction: Merge conflict when checking out the PR branch required manual resolution
Initial miscommunication where explanation was provided instead of executing the fix
Assistant initially planned to use --no-verify but was corrected by user
open_items: ORAS_SHA256 ARG must be manually updated when bumping ORAS_VERSION to a new release
PR has a failing ci/prow/security test that may need re-running after this fix (/test security)
